query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "researchclawbench: a benchmark for end-to-end autonomous scientific re" computer science
rationale: The paper is a general survey on multi-agent modeling and does not address the specific domain of "autonomous scientific research," "end-to-end scientific discovery," or the specific benchmarks (ResearchClawBench) mentioned in the user's question. It fails to meet criteria (a) or (b) as it lacks the specific mechanism (scientific research automation) or the specific empirical population (scientific agents) required, and it is not a foundational method for the specific task of autonomous science
